name: Shortcut API
what_it_does: Shortcut's (formerly Clubhouse) project management REST API for managing stories, epics, milestones, iterations, and workflows for software development teams.
use_cases: Creating and updating stories from agent-driven development workflows
Querying iteration (sprint) status and story completion for planning agents
Moving stories through workflow states (triage, in development, done)
Generating engineering velocity metrics and iteration summaries
Automating story creation from GitHub pull requests or bug reports
not_for: Teams using Jira or Linear (API migration is significant effort)
Complex enterprise workflows requiring Jira's extensibility
best_when: Your software team uses Shortcut and you need agents to automate story management, sprint tracking, or workflow automation.
avoid_when: You're not a Shortcut customer or need enterprise project management with complex customization.

auth.notes: Single API token per workspace member. Token passed as Shortcut-Token header. No fine-grained scopes — full access to workspace for that user. Tokens are long-lived.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: up to 10 users, unlimited projects, full API access
pricing.notes: Generous free tier with API access. Team plan adds advanced reporting and integrations. Good value for engineering teams.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses with message field. Common errors (not found, validation failures) include field-level details. API documentation is clean and up to date.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Story creation is not idempotent. Agents should search for existing stories before creating to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: API token has full workspace access — token leakage exposes all workspace data
Workflow state IDs are workspace-specific — must query workflow states before transitioning stories
Stories, epics, and milestones use different entity types with different endpoints — don't conflate them
Search API has limited field coverage — some complex queries require listing and client-side filtering
Webhook payloads can be large for complex stories with many fields
Archive vs delete are different operations — archived stories are not deleted
